shared_properties: Asset preserves the front-facing 128x128 cage composition.
Core palette fields stay fixed at primary #1D2B53, background #FFF1E8, and accent #FF004D.
Flat pixel rendering keeps the room calm and readable behind gameplay.
differences: This layer carries two floor states for clean and dirty readability.
The dirty state must still preserve contrast for poop targets.
The floor occupies the lower scene strip and stays static per state.

reference_images.label: cage_layout_reference
reference_images.purpose: Grounds cage framing, perch placement, and lower-floor composition.

reference_images.label: art_direction_reference
reference_images.purpose: Grounds warm household palette and front-facing pixel-art rendering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas-driven sprite entity with fixed virtual-pixel scaling.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load cage_floor_clean_dirty_2f.png via AssetServer during Loading state, then bind handles by task_id for deterministic system lookup.
runtime_notes: Use nearest-neighbor sampling and a 128x128 virtual canvas so every authored pixel remains crisp after upscale.
Drive state swaps through atlas index changes or visibility toggles instead of runtime repainting to preserve palette invariance.
Attach marker components so CareSystem, BehaviorSystem, TimeSystem, and HUD systems can target the asset without filename inference.

context.usage: Bottom floor area; can tint/texture change after cleaning.

dirty_state_notes: Dirty state includes 8-12 scattered #AB5236 pixels; do not create 2x2 blocks.
Keep the top edge of the strip (y=104) clean with no dirt pixels in the first 2 rows.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 256x24px (2 states × 128x24) with 0px spacing.
Clean state uses only #FFA300 and #FFF1E8; Dirty state adds #AB5236 specks only.
Both states tile seamlessly from x=0 to x=127 (left and right edges match).
